description:
Re-seat the three consumers as the demand SOURCES that pull type defs, then delete function_type_env (world.rs:1157).

Each consumer -- DeriveFunctionContract (contract.rs:46), dispatch param annotations (dispatch.rs:265), extern types (body.rs:477) -- WAITS on the TypeDefined facts recorded as its references in .1, then runs the pure-read resolver (.3) against the now-present hard Ty. This is the cascade @spec to FunctionContract: the contract is itself a demanded fact, and its wait on TypeDefined is what pulls DeriveTypeDef for exactly the names the spec mentions -- the same way DeriveFunctionContract sits over its MFAs. ResolvedSpecDecl and crate::specs are unchanged downstream (shared, over hard Ty). Externs: in-house extern type resolution replaces env-based ir_lower::lower_extern_signature.

Verify: spec_ok / spec_boundary / spec_violation fixtures and the contract/dispatch drive tests stay green; a contract resolves before its function body is activated (two grains: a caller reads the contract without entering the body).
title: Re-seat contract/dispatch/extern onto the resolver; delete function_type_env
